sections.heading: Deployment Overview
sections.content: NEXUS can be deployed as a raw Python application for development or compiled into a standalone executable for production environments. Production deployment requires strict hardware configuration and dependency matching.

sections.heading: System Requirements
items: OS Runtime: Windows 10/11 requires Visual C++ Redistributable (x64 and x86) installed.
Dependencies: PyQt6, opencv-python, numpy, ultralytics, pyserial, scipy, torch, torchvision.
GPU Drivers: NVIDIA GPU with CUDA Toolkit installed (verify with nvcc --version).
AI Acceleration: TensorRT installed and its lib/ folder added to the system PATH.
Hardware Drivers: CH340 USB-Serial driver downloaded from WCH and installed for ESP32 communication.

sections.heading: Deployment Methods
rows: End-user deployment without exposing source code
Building GPU-specific .engine files using TensorRT FP16 for maximum FPS

sections.heading: Building the Executable
sections.code:
# Compile NEXUS into a single executable without terminal console
pyinstaller --onefile --noconsole --icon=assets/icon.ico main.py

# Note: data/ and models/ folders must be bundled or placed next to the generated .exe to function

sections.heading: First-Launch Production Setup
sections.alertContent: Before running the engine in a new environment, three settings MUST be configured: CAM_L_IDX, CAM_R_IDX, and SERIAL_PORT. The engine will fail to open cameras or connect to hardware if these point to disconnected or occupied ports.
